title: Evgo Inc. Q3 Earnings Call: Growth Amid Challenges
text: Evgo Inc. demonstrated strong financial performance in Q3 2025, reporting $92 million in total revenue—a 37% year-over-year increase. Charging network revenues reached $56 million, up 33% from the prior year. The company achieved operational milestones: it ended the quarter with 4,590 stalls in operation, representing a 2.7x increase since 2021. Its customer base has expanded nearly fivefold since 2021, driving increased network usage. Financial highlights include adjusted EBITDA improved by $4 million compared to Q3 2024, expectation to reach breakeven adjusted EBITDA in Q4 2025, receipt of $41 million from Department of Energy loan, and $225 million commercial financing facility. Notable developments: Evgo expanded its NACS connector pilot to nearly 100 cables to accommodate Tesla drivers. The company projects $0.5 billion in adjusted EBITDA by 2029. Challenges acknowledged: Stall deployment timing has shifted with some units moving from December 2025 to January 2026. The company lowered 2025 stall deployment expectations due to strategic adjustments.
